topic: Someone discovers their mother kept every school assignment
raw_output:
[story 1]

The attic air hung thick and dusty, smelling of aged paper and forgotten things. Leo had been tasked with clearing out some old boxes, a chore he’d been putting off for weeks. Tucked inside a brittle, velvet-lined trunk, he found it: a meticulously organized stack of every single school assignment from elementary school through high school. His breath hitched in his throat. He carefully lifted the top sheet, the crisp edges feeling strangely significant under his fingertips. A tremor ran through his hands as he realized the sheer volume—years of effort, preserved. He quickly shoved the bundle back into the trunk, his heart thumping a frantic rhythm against his ribs. He needed to leave. Now.

[story 2]

"Mom, what is all this?" Maya asked, her voice tight with apprehension as she stared at the overflowing filing cabinet in the spare room. She’d been looking for old tax documents and stumbled upon a collection of meticulously labeled folders, each containing a completed essay, a perfectly drawn diagram, a graded test. A cold knot tightened in her stomach. She ran a hand over the spine of a binder labeled 'Algebra II.' The weight of responsibility, suddenly immense and unfamiliar, settled heavily on her shoulders. She backed away slowly, her eyes darting around the room, searching for an explanation that didn't feel quite right.

[story 3]

I found the box behind the seldom-used bookshelf while cleaning out the basement. It wasn't filled with junk; it was a treasure trove of paper. Every homework assignment, every project, every graded paper from my entire academic career was there, stacked with military precision. My palms started sweating immediately. I picked up a history report from middle school, the ink faded but the handwriting instantly recognizable. A strange, hollow feeling washed over me. I dropped the papers onto the dusty floor, the sound echoing loudly in the quiet house. I stood there, motionless, just trying to process the sudden, unsettling weight of this secret.
